# nullplatform_capability (Resource)

The capability resource allows you to manage nullplatform Capabilities

## Example Usage

```terraform
terraform {
  required_providers {
    nullplatform = {
      source = "nullplatform/nullplatform"
    }
  }
}

provider "nullplatform" {}

resource "nullplatform_capability" "cpu_limits" {
  name        = "CPU Limits"
  target      = "scope"
  description = "Allowed CPU configurations for scopes"

  # JSON schema describing the values this capability accepts
  definition = jsonencode({
    type = "object"
    properties = {
      cpu = {
        type = "string"
        enum = ["0.25", "0.5", "1"]
      }
    }
    required             = ["cpu"]
    additionalProperties = false
  })
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `definition` (String) JSON string containing the capability definition (a JSON schema).
- `name` (String) The name of the capability. Maximum length is 60 characters.
- `target` (String) The target of the capability.

### Optional

- `description` (String) A description of the capability. Maximum length is 2048 characters.
- `slug` (String) A unique slug for the capability. Generated from the name if not provided.
- `status` (String) The status of the capability.

### Read-Only

- `created_at` (String) The creation timestamp of the capability.
- `id` (String) The ID of this resource.
- `updated_at` (String) The last update timestamp of the capability.
